headline: Transcript: Nuclear weapons and the balance of terror
summary: Gideon Rachman talks to historian and author Serhii Plokhy
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Rachman Review podcast episode: ‘Nuclear weapons and the balance of terror’

John Burn-MurdochHi folks. I’m John Burn-Murdoch, the FT’s chief data reporter.

Sara O’ConnorAnd I’m Sarah O’Connor, the FT’s employment columnist. And John, we share a fascination for how AI is starting to change the labour market.

John Burn-MurdochTotally. Is AI going to make us all better at our jobs or lead to a wave of unemployment?

Sara O’ConnorBut we’re also a bit tired of the hype and the wild predictions.

John Burn-MurdochSo we’ve launched a newsletter, the AI Shift. Our data and reporting will tell you how AI is actually changing the world of work.

Sara O’ConnorFT Premium subscribers can sign up now at FT.com/aishift.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Gideon RachmanHello and welcome to The Rachman Review. I’m Gideon Rachman, chief Foreign Affairs commentator of the Financial Times.

This week’s podcast is about nuclear weapons and international relations. My guest is Professor
